name: Can a handyman handle bathroom repairs, or is that a remodel job?
headline: Can a handyman handle bathroom repairs, or is that a remodel job?

mainEntity.name: Can a handyman handle bathroom repairs, or is that a remodel job?
mainEntity.text: Can a handyman handle bathroom repairs, or is that a remodel job?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most handymen are comfortable with **small to medium bathroom repairs**, especially those that don’t require moving plumbing or changing the structure of the room. Common bathroom tasks a handyman can often handle include: - Replacing faucets, showerheads, and basic fixtures - Fixing or replacing toilets and toilet hardware - Caulking tubs, showers, and sinks to stop small leaks - Repairing water‑damaged drywall after a leak has been fixed - Installing towel bars, grab bars, mirrors, and medicine cabinets For **full remodels** or bigger changes, you usually need a general contractor and licensed trades. This includes: - Moving a tub, shower, toilet, or sink to a new spot - Rebuilding a shower or tub area with new tile and waterproofing - Adding new electrical circuits or major lighting changes Pitfalls: - Asking a handyman to “do it all” on a full remodel without the right licensing or permits. You may end up with hidden water or moisture problems. - Fixing only the surface (like repainting over water stains) without solving the underlying leak first. Before hiring, describe what you want in detail. If it’s mostly “fix this” or “replace that” with items in the same location, a handyman may be enough. If your plan sounds like “gut and rebuild,” you’re in remodel territory.
